summary: The GT500's 5.2L Predator supercharged V8 is a well-engineered powerhouse. The DCT transmission handles the torque well. Main concerns are heat management under track use and supercharger maintenance.

issues.name: Heat Soak Under Track Use
issues.description: The GT500 can go into thermal management mode during sustained track use, reducing power to protect the engine. The factory heat exchanger is the bottleneck.

issues.name: DCT Transmission Overheating
issues.description: The Tremec 7-speed dual-clutch transmission can overheat during aggressive driving, especially in hot weather. A transmission cooler upgrade helps significantly.

issues.name: Supercharger Belt Slip
issues.description: Under very high loads or in hot weather, the supercharger drive belt can slip, causing a momentary loss of boost. An upgraded belt or tensioner resolves it.

issues.name: MagneRide Damper Failure
issues.description: The MagneRide adaptive dampers have a finite lifespan and can leak or lose their adaptive capability over time.

issues.name: Carbon Fiber Wing Vibration
issues.description: The GT500's large carbon fiber rear wing can develop vibration at high speeds on some examples. Ford issued a TSB for wing bracket reinforcement.

buyingTips: The Predator 5.2L V8 is a strong engine — the supercharger is conservatively tuned from the factory with plenty of headroom
The DCT is the only transmission option — there is no manual GT500. Learn to love the dual-clutch
Track use requires upgrades — budget for a heat exchanger and transmission cooler if you plan to do HPDE events
Check for Carbon Fiber Handling Package — these cars are significantly more capable on track
Low production numbers mean these will likely appreciate — keep modifications reversible and maintain fastidiously
